issues.name: stripe-like-api-products
issues.description: Refactor Product and Price endpoints to match Stripe patterns
steps: [DONE] Rename route `GET /v1/subscriptions/products` to `GET /v1/products`.
[DONE] Add `GET /v1/prices` endpoint to list individual prices.
[DONE] Update `GetProducts` handler to return `ListResponse` of `ProductObject`.
[DONE] Update `GetProducts` handler to map internal models to new API structs.
[DONE] Update tests to use new routes and validate new response format.

issues.name: stripe-like-api-rest
issues.description: Consolidate user-scoped endpoints under /v1/me/ and standardize RESTful patterns
steps: [DONE] Move `GET /v1/subscriptions/active` + `GET /v1/subscriptions/history` → `GET /v1/me/subscriptions` with query params (?status=active, ?status=all)
[DONE] Move `GET /v1/subscriptions/purchases` → `GET /v1/me/payments`
[DONE] Move `POST /v1/subscriptions/cancel` → `POST /v1/me/subscriptions/cancel`
[DONE] Move `GET /v1/payment-methods` → `GET /v1/me/payment-methods` (also POST, PUT, DELETE)
[DONE] Move `GET /v1/wallet/solana/*` → `GET /v1/me/wallets/*` (all wallet endpoints)
[DONE] Move `GET /v1/notifications/*` → `GET /v1/me/notifications/*` (all notification endpoints)

[DONE] Refactor Solana payment endpoints: `POST /v1/solana/generate` → `POST /v1/payment-intents`, `POST /v1/solana/submit` → `POST /v1/payment-intents/:id/confirm`, `GET /v1/solana/check` → `GET /v1/payment-intents/:id`, `POST /v1/solana/qr` → `POST /v1/payment-intents/qr`
[DONE] Keep legacy routes for backwards compatibility
[DONE] Update all corresponding tests.

issues.name: cleanup-worker
issues.description: Add cleanup worker for expired temporary data
steps: [DONE] Identify all temporary tables/records that need cleanup (wallet challenges, payment intents, notifications, etc.)
[DONE] Create River job for periodic cleanup (CleanupExpiredDataArgs)
[DONE] Implement cleanup logic: delete expired wallet challenges, payment intents, solana transactions, notifications, idempotency requests, webhook events
[DONE] Add configurable retention periods for each data type (CleanupConfig struct with defaults)
[DONE] Schedule worker to run periodically (every hour via River PeriodicJob)
[DONE] Add metrics/logging for cleanup operations (logs deleted counts per table)
[DONE] Write tests to verify expired data is cleaned up correctly

issues.name: processor-jsonb-field
issues.description: Consolidate processor config on Price model into single JSONB 'processors' field
steps: [DONE] Add 'processors' JSONB field to Price model (map[string]map[string]string)
[DONE] Create migration to add processors column and remove old columns (006_add_processors_jsonb.up.sql)
[SKIP] Data migration not needed - starting fresh with no existing data
[DONE] Update PriceRepo to use JSONB queries (GetByNMIPlan, GetByCCBillPriceID)
[DONE] Update Subscribe handler to use price.GetNMIConfig() for NMI processor availability
[DONE] Update GenerateFlexFormURL handler to use price.GetCCBillConfig() for CCBill
[DONE] Update webhook handlers to use JSONB lookups for processor keys
[DONE] Remove old nmi_plan_id, nmi_provider, ccbill_price_id columns from Price model
[DONE] Update seed data helpers to use new processors format
[DONE] Add helper methods: GetNMIConfig(), GetCCBillConfig(), GetSolanaConfig(), SetNMIConfig(), etc.

issues.name: ccbill-upgrade-url
issues.description: Add CCBill upgrade URL generation endpoint
steps: Create POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill/upgrade-url endpoint
Accept target_price_id and validate user has active CCBill subscription
Generate CCBill upgrade FlexForm URL with user's existing subscription info
Return upgrade URL for frontend to redirect user
Document CCBill Admin setup requirements (Package ID, Package Upgrade Allowed)
Write tests for upgrade URL generation

issues.name: ccbill-upgrade-entitlements
issues.description: Update entitlements on subscription upgrade
steps: In handleUpgradeSuccess, get new product's EntitlementsSpec after ActivateWithPrice
Revoke old entitlements that aren't in the new product's spec
Grant new entitlements from the upgraded product
Use existing entitlement granting logic (from lifecycle_service or admin_subscription_service)
Write tests for entitlement updates on tier upgrade
Handle edge case: same entitlements in both tiers (extend end dates)

issues.name: unify-admin-auth
issues.description: Remove separate admin port and API key, use JWT with admin role claims instead
steps: [DONE] CURRENT: Admin API runs on separate port with API key authentication
[DONE] PROBLEM: API key is a shared secret - if stolen, attacker has full admin access with no audit trail
[DONE] Add admin role/claims check to existing JWT validation middleware (e.g., middleware.AdminRequired)
[DONE] Move admin routes from separate adminHandler to publicHandler, protected by AdminRequired middleware
[DONE] Remove BillingAPIKey from config
[DONE] Remove separate admin server/port setup
[DONE] Update admin route paths (now /v1/admin/subscriptions/:id/extend, /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements, etc.)
[DONE] Update tests to use JWT Bearer tokens with admin role instead of X-API-KEY
BENEFIT: Each admin request tied to user identity, full audit trail, no shared secrets

issues.name: admin-api-enhancements
issues.description: Add admin API endpoints for user lookup, subscription management, refunds, and entitlement management
steps: [DONE] User lookup by ID:
[DONE]   - GET /v1/admin/users/:user_id - get user's subscription + entitlements + payments in one call
[DONE] Subscription listing:
[DONE]   - GET /v1/admin/subscriptions - list/search subscriptions with filters (status, processor, user_id, date range, pagination)
[DONE]   - GET /v1/admin/subscriptions/:id/payments - payment history for a subscription
[DONE] Refunds:
[DONE]   - POST /v1/admin/payments/:id/refund - issue refund (creates negative payment entry for audit trail)
[DONE]   - Track refund status and amount in payments table (via RefundedPaymentID linkage)
[DONE] Entitlement management:
[DONE]   - POST /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements - grant entitlement manually (with optional days for end_at)
[DONE]   - DELETE /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements/:id - revoke entitlement immediately (sets revoked_at, revoke_reason)
Write tests for all new endpoints

issues.name: cli-duplicate-detection
issues.description: CLI command to detect duplicate charges and multiple active subscriptions for the same user
steps: Create cmd/billing-cli/main.go with cobra CLI framework
Add 'duplicates' command with subcommands:
- 'billing-cli duplicates charges' - find users charged twice in same billing period
- Query payments grouped by user_id, price_id, month
- Flag any user with >1 successful payment for same price in same 30-day window
- Output: user_id, payment_ids, amounts, dates, recommendation (refund which one)
- 'billing-cli duplicates subscriptions' - find users with multiple active subscriptions
- Query subscriptions WHERE status='active' GROUP BY user_id HAVING count > 1
- Output: user_id, subscription_ids, prices, start_dates, recommendation (cancel which one)
Add --format flag for output (table, json, csv)
Add --dry-run flag (default) vs --fix flag for auto-remediation (with confirmation prompt)
Add date range filters (--since, --until) to limit search scope
Write tests for duplicate detection logic

issues.name: cli-orphan-entitlements
issues.description: CLI command to detect users with active entitlements that should have expired
steps: Add 'orphans' command to billing-cli:
- 'billing-cli orphans entitlements' - find users with premium but no valid source
Query logic:
- Get all users with active entitlements (end_at IS NULL OR end_at > now, revoked_at IS NULL)
- For each, check if they have a valid source:
- source_type='subscription' → verify subscription is still active and not expired
- source_type='one_off' → verify payment exists and end_at hasn't passed
- source_type='admin' → SKIP (intentionally granted, don't flag)
- Flag entitlements where source is invalid/expired but entitlement still active
Output columns:
- user_id, entitlement_id, entitlement_name, source_type, source_id
- source_status (e.g., 'subscription_cancelled', 'subscription_expired', 'payment_refunded')
- entitlement_start, expected_end, recommendation
Add --include-admin flag to also audit admin-granted entitlements (off by default)
Add --format flag (table, json, csv)
Add --fix flag to auto-revoke orphaned entitlements (with confirmation prompt)
Write tests for orphan detection logic

issues.name: admin-user-search
issues.description: Sophisticated user search for admin dashboard
steps: Design search API:
- GET /v1/admin/users?q=...&filters... - search users with billing data
Search fields:
- email (partial match from subscriptions/payments)
- user_id (exact match)
- processor_subscription_id (exact match for support lookups)
- transaction_id (exact match for payment support)
Filters:
- has_subscription=true/false
- subscription_status=active/cancelled/past_due/etc
- processor=nmi/ccbill/solana
- has_entitlement=premium/etc
- created_after, created_before (date range)
Sorting:
- sort_by=newest/oldest/last_payment/subscription_start
Pagination:
- page, page_size, cursor-based pagination for large results
Performance:
- Add database indexes for search fields
- Consider full-text search for email
- Rate limit search queries
NOTE: Billing service searches its own data. Full user profile search (username, etc) lives in main doujins API.

issues.name: admin-rate-limiting
issues.description: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints to limit blast radius of compromised JWT
steps: PROBLEM: If admin JWT is leaked, attacker could cancel/refund thousands of users before detection
Implement per-admin-user rate limiting using Redis (keyed by admin user ID from JWT)
Define rate limits for destructive operations: e.g., 5 cancellations/minute, 10/hour, 50/day
Define rate limits for bulk operations: e.g., 3 extend operations/minute
On rate limit exceeded: lock out admin for extended period (e.g., 1 hour) and alert
Add alerting/notification when rate limits are approached (e.g., 80% threshold)
Create admin rate limit middleware that wraps destructive endpoints
Allow super-admin or manual override to unlock rate-limited admin if legitimate
Log all rate limit events for security audit
BENEFIT: Limits blast radius - attacker can only affect ~5-10 users before getting locked out

issues.name: prefixed-resource-ids
issues.description: Add Stripe-like prefixes to resource IDs in API layer (prod_xxx, price_xxx, sub_xxx)
steps: NOTE: Prefixes should only exist in the API layer, NOT in the database. Database keeps clean UUIDs for indexing/joins.
Create pkg/api/id.go with helper functions: FormatProductID(uuid) -> 'prod_xxx', FormatPriceID(uuid) -> 'price_xxx', FormatSubscriptionID(uuid) -> 'sub_xxx', FormatPaymentID(uuid) -> 'pay_xxx'
Create ParseProductID(string) -> uuid that strips 'prod_' prefix and validates
Create ParsePriceID, ParseSubscriptionID, ParsePaymentID similarly
Update response conversion functions (ProductToAPI, PriceToAPI, etc.) to use FormatXxxID()
Update request handlers to use ParseXxxID() when accepting IDs from clients
Return clear error if ID prefix doesn't match expected resource type (e.g., 'prod_xxx' passed to subscription endpoint)
Update tests to use prefixed IDs in requests and expect them in responses
BENEFIT: Easier debugging, self-documenting IDs, matches Stripe pattern

issues.name: structured-error-responses
issues.description: Standardize error responses to match Stripe's error format
steps: CURRENT: Errors return simple {message: 'error text'}
TARGET: Errors return {error: {type, code, message, param}} like Stripe
Create pkg/api/error.go with ErrorResponse and ErrorDetails structs
Define error types: 'invalid_request_error', 'authentication_error', 'authorization_error', 'api_error', 'card_error', 'idempotency_error'
Define error codes: 'missing_param', 'invalid_param', 'resource_not_found', 'authentication_required', 'insufficient_permissions', etc.
Update Request.ErrorJSON() in internal/handlers/request.go to accept structured error info
Create helper functions: InvalidParamError(param, message), NotFoundError(resource), AuthRequiredError(), etc.
Update handlers to use structured error helpers instead of plain strings
Update tests to expect structured error format
BENEFIT: Clients can programmatically handle errors by type/code, better UX

issues.name: solana-devnet-integration-tests
issues.description: Add real Solana devnet integration tests that move test SOL/tokens on-chain
steps: SETUP COMPLEXITY: Medium-high. Requires funded devnet wallet and handling rate limits/faucet availability.
Create test wallet keypair specifically for integration tests (store in config or env)
Fund test wallet via Solana devnet faucet (solana airdrop) - may need retry logic due to rate limits
Create test for full payment flow: generate intent -> sign transaction -> submit -> verify on-chain
Use devnet RPC endpoint (https://api.devnet.solana.com or Helius/QuickNode devnet)
Test SOL transfer: create intent for X lamports, sign with test wallet, submit, verify balance changes
Test SPL token transfer: create test SPL token on devnet, test token payment flow
Handle devnet instability: add retries, longer timeouts, skip tests if devnet unavailable
Consider using localnet (solana-test-validator) for faster/more reliable tests as alternative
Add -tags=devnet build tag to separate from unit tests (expensive/slow to run)
Document devnet wallet setup and faucet requirements in README

issues.name: manage-subscription-uuid-panic
issues.description: Admin manage endpoints call uuid.MustParse on request payloads; malformed UUIDs crash the server instead of returning an error.
steps: Update ManageSubscriptionService to validate UUID inputs with Parse + error handling (no MustParse).
Propagate user-friendly 400/422 errors from handlers for bad UUIDs.
Add tests covering malformed subscription IDs for update/extend admin routes to ensure no panic.

issues.name: payment-method-pagination-reset
issues.description: ListPaymentMethods sets defaults after parsing user-supplied page/page_size, overriding client pagination.
steps: Reorder pagination handling so defaults are applied before parsing query params (or only when absent).
Simplify BindQuery usage to avoid manual overrides and duplicate validation.
Add handler tests confirming user-provided page/page_size are honored.

issues.name: redis-password-ignored-nonprod
issues.description: Redis client only sets password in production; staging/dev with protected Redis fail and rate limiting silently falls back to in-memory.
steps: Always apply Redis password when provided, regardless of env.
Adjust log messaging to reflect actual auth usage and warn on failed connections.
Add tests (or injectable opts) to ensure password is respected outside prod.

issues.name: admin-actions-ignore-request-context
issues.description: Admin status/extend handlers run with context.Background(), so work continues after client disconnects and ignores request timeouts.
steps: Use r.Request.Context() (or a derived timeout) in manage handlers instead of context.Background().
Audit ManageSubscriptionService entry points to propagate the provided context to DB calls.
Add tests ensuring cancellation/timeout on request context stops handler work.

issues.name: remove-wallet-verification-requirement
issues.description: Remove mandatory wallet verification before Solana payments - it's unnecessary since transaction signatures already prove ownership
steps: CURRENT: User must link+verify wallet via challenge/sign before making a payment
PROBLEM: This is an unnecessary step - the transaction signature itself proves wallet ownership
Update CreatePaymentIntent to accept any wallet address (not just verified ones)
Update ConfirmPaymentIntent to verify the signed transaction's fee payer matches the claimed wallet
Keep wallet linking endpoints for optional UX convenience (remember wallets) but don't require verification for payments
Update GeneratePayment handler similarly
Remove isVerified checks from payment flows
Update tests to reflect new behavior (payments work without pre-verification)
BENEFIT: Simpler UX - users can pay immediately without linking wallet first

issues.name: solana-pay-transaction-request
issues.description: Implement Solana Pay Transaction Request spec (server-built transactions via HTTPS endpoint)
steps: CURRENT: We only support Transfer Requests (solana:<recipient>?amount=X) where wallet builds the transaction
TARGET: Also support Transaction Requests (solana:https://api.example.com/pay) where server builds the transaction
Create GET /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint returning { label, icon } per spec
Create POST /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint accepting { account } and returning { transaction, message? }
Server builds the transaction with the user's account as signer (not fee payer if we want sponsored txs)
Return base64-encoded serialized transaction for wallet to sign and broadcast
Update CreatePaymentIntentQR to optionally return transaction request URL instead of transfer request URL
Add config option to choose between transfer vs transaction request mode
BENEFIT: Enables complex transactions (swaps, multi-instruction), sponsored fees, and server-side transaction building
Write tests for transaction request flow

issues.name: solana-sponsored-transactions
issues.description: Allow merchant to pay Solana transaction fees so users don't need SOL in their wallet
steps: CURRENT: User is fee payer - must have SOL for transaction fees (~0.000005 SOL)
TARGET: Merchant pays fees - users can pay with just USDC/tokens, no SOL needed
Create separate 'fee payer' wallet (distinct from recipient wallet to limit blast radius)
Add fee_payer_wallet and fee_payer_private_key to Solana config (or use KMS reference)
Integrate with AWS KMS or GCP Cloud HSM for secure private key storage (never store raw key in config)
Update BuildPaymentTransaction to set merchant as fee payer (account[0])
Server signs transaction first with fee payer key, then returns partially-signed tx to user
User signs as token sender, transaction now has both signatures
Add rate limiting specifically for sponsored transactions to prevent abuse/draining
Add monitoring/alerting for fee payer wallet balance (alert when low)
Add config flag to enable/disable sponsored transactions (default off)
Consider using Helius or Jito for fee sponsorship as simpler alternative to self-managed keys
TRADEOFF: Adds complexity and security burden (private key on server) but improves UX significantly
Write tests for sponsored transaction flow

issues.name: remove-nmi-from-codebase
issues.description: Remove 'nmi' references from codebase - NMI should be an invisible implementation detail, not visible in API/routes/database
steps: Create NMI-backed processors set in one central place (config or constants)
- Define var NMIBackedProcessors = map[string]bool{'mobius': true} or load from config
- This is the ONLY place that knows which processors use NMI gateway
- Adding 'acme' processor = add config block + add to this set, zero code changes
Remove ProcessorNMI constant usage from switch statements
- Replace `case ProcessorNMI:` with `case isNMIBacked(processor)` or similar
- subscription.go Subscribe() should switch on 'mobius', 'ccbill', 'solana' directly
Update webhook routing to check NMIBackedProcessors set
- `/webhooks/mobius` and `/webhooks/acme` both route to handleNMIWebhook
- Check `if NMIBackedProcessors[provider]` instead of `if provider == 'nmi'`
Remove 'nmi' as valid webhook route - only accept processor names (mobius, ccbill, solana)
Audit database: processor column should store 'mobius' not 'nmi'
- If any records have processor='nmi', migrate them to 'mobius'
Consider removing Gateway field from models entirely
- Currently stores 'nmi' for mobius subscriptions - but this is redundant
- If processor='mobius', we KNOW gateway=nmi, no need to store it
- Or repurpose Gateway for multi-tenant config (e.g., different NMI merchant accounts)
Update NMI client map to be keyed by processor name
- Already done: NMIClients['mobius'] returns the NMI client for mobius
Update config structure for clarity
- config.nmi.mobius.api_key, config.nmi.acme.api_key etc.
- Each processor that uses NMI has its own config block under 'nmi'
Remove testdata/webhooks/nmi folder - rename to testdata/webhooks/mobius
Update replay service to use processor names not 'nmi'
Update documentation to clarify terminology
GOAL: 'nmi' appears ONLY in internal NMI client code, never in routes/API/database/config keys

issues.name: simplify-solana-pay-architecture
issues.description: Simplify Solana Pay to use Redis for pending state, server-side polling, and eliminate the SolanaPaymentIntent model

spec.overview: Replace the complex SolanaPaymentIntent state machine with a simpler architecture: Redis for ephemeral pending payments, a background goroutine that polls Solana every 500ms for confirmations, and Postgres only for confirmed payments.
current_problems: SolanaPaymentIntent table mirrors blockchain state unnecessarily
Multiple endpoints for what should be simple operations
Frontend polling triggers blockchain checks (fragile if frontend disconnects)

POST /v1/solana/pay.response: { url, reference, amount, token_amount, token, expires_at }
behavior: Generate random 32-byte reference (base58 encoded)
Calculate token amount from price using current quote
Build solana: Transfer Request URL with reference
Store in Redis HASH: solana_pay:{ref} → { user_id, price_id, amount, token, recipient, created_at } with 15min TTL
Add reference to Redis SET 'pending_solana_payments'
Return URL for frontend to display as QR
Check Postgres for Payment with this reference - if found, return confirmed + payment_id + signature
Check Redis for solana_pay:{ref} - if not found, return expired
Otherwise return pending (poller handles actual blockchain checking)

poller.name: SolanaPaymentPoller
poller.type: Goroutine with 500ms ticker (NOT a River job)
poller.why_not_river: River jobs have overhead, 500ms polling needs a lightweight goroutine
poller.startup: go StartSolanaPoller(ctx, state) in main.go alongside HTTP server
behavior: Ticker fires every 500ms
SMEMBERS pending_solana_payments → get all pending references
If empty, continue (SMEMBERS on empty set is ~0.1ms, negligible)
Batch RPC: send single HTTP request with N getSignaturesForAddress queries
For each reference with signature found:
- Fetch full transaction via getTransaction RPC
- Verify amount, recipient, and token match expected values from Redis HASH
- Create Payment record in Postgres (idempotent on signature)
- Grant entitlements to user
- SREM from pending_solana_payments SET
- DEL solana_pay:{ref} HASH
For each reference with no signature:
- Check if expired (created_at > 15min ago or HASH missing)
- If expired, SREM from SET and continue
- Otherwise, still pending, do nothing
poller.latency: ~500-900ms from user signing tx to confirmed in our system (400ms slot time + 0-500ms poll interval)

redis_structure.pending_solana_payments: SET of reference strings currently being polled
redis_structure.solana_pay:{reference}: HASH { user_id, price_id, amount, token, recipient, created_at } with 15min TTL

flow: When status=confirmed → redirect to success page

what_to_keep: Payment model and table (confirmed payments only)
SolanaTransaction model (audit trail of on-chain txs)
Transaction verification logic (amount, recipient, token checks)
benefits: Simpler mental model: Redis for pending, Postgres for confirmed
Server-side polling ensures payments processed even if frontend disconnects
Snappy UX: 500ms polling catches payments almost instantly
Efficient: batch RPC means 1 HTTP request regardless of pending count
Easier to reason about and debug
